forecast-period.text: A low pressure trough will move across SA on Thursday followed by a cold front in the south. The trough and front will clear to the east during Friday morning. Another trough will move across waters to the south during Friday then a high pressure system will move across waters south of WA on Saturday to be centred over the Bight on Sunday. This high will move slowly eastwards early next week and a weak cold front will move over southern coastal areas.
Wind and wave forecasts are averages. Wind gusts can be 40 per cent stronger than the forecast, and stronger still in squalls and thunderstorms. Maximum waves can be twice the forecast height.
For latest warnings go to www.bom.gov.au, subscribe to RSS feeds, call 1300 659 210* or listen for warnings on relevant TV and radio broadcasts.
* Calls to 1300 numbers cost around 27.5c incl. GST, higher from mobiles or public phones. Marine forecasts are also available on VHF and HF radio. View the radio schedule on www.bom.gov.au/marine
Check the latest Coastal Waters Forecasts for information on wind, wave and weather conditions for neighbouring coastal zones.

area.description: Lower West Coast: Elliston to Cape Carnot

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Thursday for Lower West Coast
forecast-period.text: West to southwesterly 20 to 25 knots, reaching up to 30 knots offshore.
2 metres.
Southwesterly 3 metres.
Partly cloudy.

forecast-period.warning-summary: Strong Wind Warning for Friday for Lower West Coast
forecast-period.text: Southwesterly 20 to 30 knots tending west to southwesterly 20 to 25 knots before dawn then becoming southwesterly 20 to 30 knots in the early afternoon.
1.5 to 2.5 metres.
Southwesterly 3 metres, increasing to 3 to 4 metres during the morning.
Cloudy. 70% chance of showers.
Southwesterly 15 to 20 knots.
1.5 to 2 metres, decreasing below 1.5 metres around midday.
Southwesterly 3 to 4 metres.
Cloudy.
Southwesterly 10 to 15 knots turning southerly below 10 knots during the afternoon.
Around 1 metre.
Southwesterly 2 to 4 metres, tending southerly 2 to 4 metres during the morning.
Southerly 3 to 4 metres.
Cloudy.
